findings.evidence: Line 18 applies `@[expose]` to the whole section, while lines 68-146 already add API lemmas for most definitions.
findings.fix: Use `public section` and expose only declarations that downstream code genuinely must unfold; add characteristic lemmas instead of making bodies part of the public interface.
findings.issue: `@[expose] public section` exposes every definition body in this file, including `ExchangeableAt`, `FullyExchangeable`, `Contractable`, `pathLaw`, and helper maps.

findings.evidence: `rg` shows only the definition at lines 60-61; the file has `ExchangeableAt.permute` at lines 138-141 and `Contractable.map` at lines 143-146 but no `FullyExchangeable.*` theorem.
findings.fix: Add a characteristic theorem such as `FullyExchangeable.permute : h -> finiteBlockLaw μ X π = pathLaw μ X` and, if bodies are hidden, an iff/restatement for the defining law.
findings.issue: `FullyExchangeable` has no named eliminator/restatement analogous to `ExchangeableAt.permute` or `Contractable.map`, so users need the exposed body to use a hypothesis.

findings.evidence: Line 28 declares arbitrary `{ι : Type*}` and line 31 defines `finiteBlockLaw ... (k : ι -> ℕ) : Measure (ι -> α)` with no finiteness assumption, while the roadmap describes finite-dimensional laws under permutations of `Fin n`.
findings.fix: Either restrict the public finite-law primitive to `Fin n`/finite index data, or rename this generic helper to a non-finite `blockLaw` and keep the finite `prefixLaw`/finite API as the advertised surface.
findings.issue: `finiteBlockLaw` is public but is indexed by arbitrary `ι`, so it is not actually finite and overlaps the separate `pathLaw` case when `ι = ℕ`.

summary: The new exchangeability surface is close, but it currently relies on exposing all bodies and leaves a misleading over-general finite-law primitive public. Add the missing characteristic API and narrow or rename the public block-law interface.
